behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at the embedding model output contract. A stubbed embedding can always return the expected size and hide real breakage (e.g., model/version dimension drift). This behavior depends on a real AI/ML call output property, so Integration is too low; Workflow is not needed because this checks a single model-dependent step.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with a fixed query, read the live index dimension from the vector DB schema/config, and assert exact dimension match. Fail if mismatched or if model response shape is malformed.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: The failure typically appears at the real vector DB boundary (index/query semantics, score sorting behavior, API response ordering). In-process mocks often always return sorted fixtures and hide DB-specific ordering bugs. No LLM-output-quality dependency, so Agentic is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real vector DB test index with controlled vectors, issue a real similarity query through the API/search layer, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected nearest-neighbor order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly about model judgment quality/effect ('nuanced intent' and changed order). Mocked reranker outputs would trivially pass and hide real failures in relevance reasoning. Not Workflow because only one agentic decision (rerank) is being validated, not multiple agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: For a curated nuanced-query dataset, run real retrieval then real LLM reranking; assert at least one position change from raw similarity order and verify changes are consistent with expected intent labels/tie-break criteria.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: This behavior evaluates relevance quality under natural-language query semantics ('no close matches' vs noise), which depends on real embedding/model behavior plus retrieval thresholding. Deterministic stubs can mask semantic false positives. Workflow is not required because this does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real embedding model and real search backend on a corpus intentionally unrelated to the query set; assert API returns empty results (or all candidates below configured cutoff and filtered out).

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-infrastructure performance boundary (network calls, service stack, DB/API latency, process/runtime effects). Mocking external calls hides true latency distributions. Higher level rejected: behavior text is strictly latency SLA ('below 2 seconds for the median case'), not model output correctness/quality, so Agentic is not strictly required as the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end benchmark against deployed-like environment with real HTTP requests and real dependencies; collect latency over a representative query set and assert p50 < 2.0s with warmup and controlled load.
